method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov/robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted www.nyc.gov/site/veterans (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace) and resolved the VetConnectNYC intake linked from the services page to a third-party host, nyc.veteranportal.combinedarms.us — the Combined Arms 'Military Resource Portal' (Next.js on CloudFront; x-powered-by: Next.js). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Veterans' Services (DVS)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 7 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: DVS is a connective agency with a vendor service layer: it points veterans to a coordinated-care network via VetConnectNYC, which runs on the third-party Combined Arms 'Military Resource Portal' (nyc.veteranportal.combinedarms.us) — no API; DVS Care Coordinators process requests manually within 3-5 business days.

DVS is unusually open: 7 NYC Open Data datasets cover not only reference directories (DVS Resource Map, NYC Veteran Owned Businesses) but de-identified SERVICE data — assistance requests (with a 'VetConnectNYC (Y/N)' flag), cases, client demographics, and historical request processing.

But the live referral is locked: the thing a veteran actually does — submit a VetConnectNYC request and get connected to housing, benefits, VA claims, health, employment, food, or legal help — has no machine-readable contract at all; it lives only in the Combined Arms web form.
Veteran/client data is published only de-identified/aggregate (DVS Clients); DVS never exposes an identifiable veteran record to Open Data.
Vendor correction: VetConnectNYC is Combined Arms, NOT Unite Us (the assignment's tentative guess). See tech-stack.md.
Net-new write surface: make a VetConnectNYC referral (ServiceReferral) — connect a veteran to services — the core resident transaction, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 7 DVS datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DVS API — and unusually, it reaches into the de-identified service layer (assistance requests, cases, clients), not just reference data.

apis_observed.owner: Combined Arms (vendor); DVS Care Coordinators work the queue
apis_observed.note: The transactional layer — a veteran submits the VetConnectNYC Request Form to be connected to services. 'CA - Military Resource Portal', Next.js on CloudFront. No JSON/OpenAPI; requests processed manually within 3-5 business days.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
redirects to www.combinedarms.us; the coordinated-care network behind VetConnectNYC.

fruit.name: DVS Resource Map
fruit.notes: Curated directory of veteran-serving resources and providers. Keyed on DVS_RES_ID; service, service details, walk-in flag, hours, phone/email/website, address, borough.

fruit.name: NYC Veteran Owned Businesses
fruit.notes: Geocoded directory of veteran-owned businesses with the full NYC geography spine (BBL, BIN, NTA, council/community district, census tract, police precinct) and certifications. Also keyed on DVS_RES_ID.

fruit.name: Assistance Requests
opendata_match: Historical DVS Client Requests Received and Processed 44f4-mjxy (14c)
Historical Veteran Peer Coordinator (VPC) Program Moves davn-rbxj (15c)
fruit.notes: De-identified intake records — the published, downstream twin of a live VetConnectNYC referral. Carries the 'VetConnectNYC (Y/N)' flag, 'Referral Made To?', engagement level/method, issue/reason, branch, discharge status, borough/council district. Never identifies the individual.

fruit.name: Cases
opendata_match: Department of Veterans' Services Cases pw4e-vms3 (9c)
fruit.notes: De-identified case records — the work Care Coordinators do after a request/referral: service type/subtype, case status, outcome resolution, 'Started as Assistance Request', 'Case is Referred'.

fruit.name: Client demographics (aggregate)
opendata_match: Department of Veterans' Services Clients idat-aemv (12c)
fruit.notes: De-identified client demographics — service era, branch, discharge type, race/ethnicity, gender, marital status, gross monthly income band. AGGREGATE ONLY. No identifiable veteran record is ever published (privacy by design).

fruit.name: Connect to services / VA claims
fruit.notes: The general 'get connected to services' front door, including VA benefits/claims assistance — routes to the VetConnectNYC Request Form (Combined Arms). No API. An alternative net-new write surface.

fruit.name: Housing / homelessness help
fruit.notes: Request housing or homelessness assistance for a veteran — routed through VetConnectNYC to a DVS Care Coordinator. Phone/web-form only; no API. The urgent path of the net-new write surface.

fruit.name: Make a VetConnectNYC referral
fruit.notes: The single most important veteran interaction — connect a veteran (self, family, or provider) to DVS services and track the referral. No API; lives only in the third-party Combined Arms 'Military Resource Portal' web form and a manual 3-5 business-day DVS Care Coordinator queue. Its only Open Data twin is the de-identified DVS Assistance Requests (jup5-7fik). The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

meta.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' platform (Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace) for the info site; the care-coordination service layer is VetConnectNYC, a third-party vendor portal on Combined Arms' 'Military Resource Portal' (Next.js on CloudFront) — a web form, no API.
meta.netnew: ServiceReferral — make a VetConnectNYC care-coordination referral connecting a veteran to services (housing, benefits, VA claims, health, employment, education, food, legal), routed to DVS Care Coordinators with an urgent path to a Veteran Resource Center.
meta.tagline: DVS publishes even its de-identified service data as open data, but the veteran's actual referral runs through a third-party vendor form with no API.
meta.headline: NYC DVS is unusually open — it publishes not just its resource directory but de-identified assistance requests, cases, and client demographics — yet the core transaction, a VetConnectNYC care-coordination referral, is a manual vendor web form on Combined Arms with no API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; no DVS-owned application platform, the service layer is a third-party vendor portal (Combined Arms / VetConnectNYC).
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No site or content API; nothing machine-readable on nyc.gov/site/veterans.
scorecard_notes.openData: 7 open Socrata datasets — unusually deep for a small agency, reaching into de-identified service data, but modest in count and partly historical.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core resource/business/assistance-request/case entities are modeled and open; client data is aggregate/de-identified only.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — every referral is a Combined Arms web form worked manually within 3-5 business days.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no structured intake an agent could call.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Reference and de-identified service data are DVS-labeled and open, but the live transaction layer runs on an out-of-city third-party vendor DVS does not own.
